question: What are the main functions of the RCS (Reactor Coolant System)?

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core using natural circulation and interfaces with several other systems for safety and operational control, including degasification lines and emergency core cooling. Its pressure-retaining parts help maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, preventing the release of radionuclides.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications, and highlights corrosion protection measures such as weld cladding with stainless steel and nickel-based alloys.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using installed instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is located, with the leakage rate determined by RCS inventory balance and compared to the containment evacuation system (CES) flow; the detection systems are reliable and support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV forming the outer pressure boundary and the SG tubes and welds contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV) is a key component that houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer, serving as a primary barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its materials are designed and fabricated according to stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s operational lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the methodology and regulatory compliance for establishing pressure-temperature operational limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes how these limits are calculated using ASME and NRC guidelines, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides the resulting limit curves and values to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

edges.description: Section A provides a general description of the RCS's functions, while Section B specifies the detailed design and materials of a key RCS component (the pressurizer), thereby elaborating on the system described in Section A.

Section A lists the major components of the RCS, including steam generators and valves, while Section B describes the main functions these components serve, such as degasification and maintaining pressure boundary integrity, thus Section A specifies the physical elements that enable the functions outlined in Section B.
